project_overview.name: ITAM (IT Asset Management)
project_overview.structure: Full-stack application with Node.js/Fastify API and Next.js frontend
project_overview.status: Advanced MVP with core functionality complete

auth.notes: Well-implemented with comprehensive audit logging for auth events

gaps: Missing audit trail for create/update operations
No insertAuditEvent calls despite having approval events
Only used for lifecycle transitions currently
approvals.implementation_pattern: Uses insertApprovalEvent for internal tracking instead of insertAuditEvent

features: Scope version lifecycle (DRAFT -> SUBMITTED -> APPROVED -> ACTIVE)
Full frontend UI for both scope and context
gaps: Missing audit trail for governance events
No insertAuditEvent despite significant state changes
governance.rbac_implementation: Advanced - checked in scope.service.js and context.service.js via getRoleCodes()

lifecycle.notes: Tightly coupled with approvals service - transitions triggered only when approvals are executed

config.notes: Read-only configuration service - no create/update operations

gaps: Only routes file - no service or repo layer
Only routes file - no service or repo layer

implementation_details: JWT includes tenant_id extracted in requestContext plugin
All service functions receive tenantId as parameter
Tenant context enforced in request plugin with fallback for dev mode

NOT_implemented_in: approvals - has insertApprovalEvent but not insertAuditEvent
governance - no audit despite scope activation changes
lifecycle - no audit despite state transitions
iam - no audit for role changes
audit_trail.function_location: src/lib/audit.js - insertAuditEvent(app, { tenantId, actor, action, entityType, entityId, payload })

implementation_pattern: Checked in service functions via mustHaveAnyRole() checks
Some modules have advanced role checking (governance)
implemented_in: governance - scope and context services check specific roles
NOT_explicitly_checked_in: assets - loads roles but doesn't enforce
auth - authentication only, no role checks needed

error_handling.pattern: Custom error objects with statusCode, code, and optional details

validation.frontend: Fastify routes use @sinclair/typebox for schema validation
validation.business_logic: Inconsistent - some modules validate thoroughly, others minimal

missing_frontend_for_modules: iam - no dedicated UI (roles managed elsewhere?)
lifecycle - lifecycle management via approvals UI
frontend_completeness.coverage: ~75% of backend modules have frontend presence

version_scale_definition.1.0: Core business logic complete (CRUD for major entities)

completed_milestones: ✓ 0.1 - Multi-tenant authentication with JWT and role support
✓ 0.2 - CRUD for core entities (assets, departments, locations, identities)
✓ 0.3 - Complete multi-tenant isolation in all queries
✓ 1.0 - Full CRUD operations for all major entities
✓ 1.1 - Approval workflow system for lifecycle transitions
✓ 1.2 - Governance framework with scope and context versioning
in_progress: ⚠ 1.3 - Audit logging (implemented for some modules, ~35% coverage needed: assets, approvals, documents, evidence, governance, lifecycle, iam)
not_started: ❌ 1.4 - Full RBAC enforcement (partially done, needs completion in: assets, approvals, documents, evidence, lifecycle)
❌ 1.5 - Advanced features (search refinement, bulk operations, compliance reporting)
❌ 1.6 - Production hardening (rate limiting, caching, comprehensive error handling)

critical.impact: Cannot track who made what changes in ~65% of the system
critical.recommendation: Add insertAuditEvent calls to all create/update/delete operations across remaining modules

affected_modules: assets - routes allow access but service doesn't check roles
critical.impact: Users may have access to operations they shouldn't
critical.recommendation: Add role checking middleware/guards to all protected endpoints

critical.gap: Missing implementations in ownership and masterdata modules
critical.details: Only routes files exist, no service/repo layers
critical.impact: These modules are likely non-functional or stubs
critical.recommendation: Complete implementation or remove if not needed

moderate.gap: Lifecycle module has no dedicated repo layer
moderate.impact: Logic is scattered between lifecycle.service.js and approval flows
moderate.recommendation: Extract lifecycle database operations into lifecycle.repo.js

moderate.gap: No comprehensive input validation in all modules
examples_missing: Assets - missing validation for asset_tag uniqueness
Departments - missing validation for department code uniqueness
moderate.recommendation: Add validation middleware for common patterns (unique codes, required fields, enum values)

moderate.gap: Admin-masters service exists but no routes are registered
moderate.impact: Admin masters functionality may not be accessible
moderate.recommendation: Create admin-masters routes or integrate into admin-config routes

moderate.gap: Permission model is role-based but unclear which roles can do what
moderate.impact: Role assignments may be arbitrary without documented rules

minor.gap: Config module is read-only with no update endpoints
minor.impact: Tenants cannot configure page sizes or UI settings
minor.recommendation: Add update endpoints for configuration values

minor.gap: No batch operations for any module
minor.recommendation: Add optional batch endpoints for common operations

minor.impact: Only basic 'q' filter on some entities
minor.recommendation: Add advanced filtering and full-text search

architecture: Clean separation of concerns with repo/service/routes pattern
Consistent error handling with custom error objects
Well-implemented multi-tenant isolation at the database query level
JWT-based stateless authentication is secure and scalable
Request context plugin effectively propagates tenant and user info
code_quality: Good naming conventions and code organization
Audit function exists but not consistently used - suggests refactoring effort was incomplete
RBAC pattern is emerging but inconsistently applied - suggests ongoing development
No apparent use of ORM - raw SQL queries with parameterized queries (good for security)
Error handling is robust with proper HTTP status codes
testing_readiness: No visible test files in provided structure - testing setup not yet visible
Schema validation with @sinclair/typebox provides some type safety
Clear service/repo boundaries make unit testing easier
security_notes: ⚠ Dev mode allows requests without token - fine for development but must be strictly enforced in production
⚠ Some operations are not checking roles - could allow unauthorized access
feature_maturity: Assets module is complete with full CRUD and frontend
Governance framework is the most sophisticated feature with version control and approval flow
Approval workflow for lifecycle transitions is well-integrated
Approval system uses separate approval_events table instead of audit_events - architectural decision unclear
technical_debt: Audit trail implementation is inconsistent - major refactoring needed
RBAC enforcement is selective - needs standardization across all modules
Some modules (ownership, masterdata) appear to be stubs
Admin-masters service not exposed via routes
No visible job queue for async operations

consistency.file_naming: Mostly consistent (routes.js, service.js, repo.js) with exception: admin-config.repo.js.js
consistency.error_response_format: Consistent: {ok: false, error: {code, message, details}, meta: {request_id}}
consistency.database_patterns: Consistent tenant_id filtering in all queries
consistency.export_patterns: Default export for routes, named exports for services/repos

immediate_priorities.approach: Add insertAuditEvent to all create/update/delete operations
Create middleware for role checking, apply consistently
Either complete implementations or remove if not needed

medium_term: Add batch operation endpoints for bulk updates
Implement response caching for read-heavy operations
Add pagination to all list endpoints (currently only assets has it)
long_term: Implement workflow engine for custom approval processes
Add reporting and compliance audit trail dashboards

summary.overall_status: Advanced MVP at version 1.2 - Core functionality complete with governance framework, but audit logging and RBAC enforcement incomplete
summary.production_readiness: 75% - Core features work, but security (RBAC), compliance (audit), and some functionality gaps need closure
strengths: Most modules have complete CRUD with frontend
summary.estimated_effort_to_production: 2-3 weeks for critical gaps, 4-6 weeks for full hardening
